_doc: Immutable allowlist for the Meta-Harness proposer. MetaHarnessProtectedSections.swift loads this; any candidate bundle that violates an invariant is BLOCKED before benchmarking (and before any PR). This file is the authoritative SUPERSET of the Swift _fallback_only minimal set — if this file is missing/corrupt, the binary's frozen minimal invariants still apply, so a bad content push can never OPEN the gate. Rules: forbidEdit = no changed file may match this glob; regexMustRetain = if a baseline file matches `pattern`, the candidate version of that file must still match it; regexForbidNew = if a candidate file matches `pattern` and its baseline did not, that is a violation. glob: a path ending in '/' is a prefix; '*' matches every file; otherwise an exact relative path. ASCII only.

invariants.reason: Self-protection: the proposer must never modify the Meta-Harness's own brain (config, proposer prompt, invariants, objectives).
The sibling self-learn loop's policy is out of scope for harness-state optimization.
The universal-wisdom layer carries severity:critical + offline-safety rules. A recipe that included it must keep it — the proposer may retune its budget/priority but not delete it.
Offline-safety: harness content must not introduce CDN/network references. Generated apps must stay offline.
No code-execution or prompt-injection smuggled into a recipe option or relevance rule.

The relevance gate's conservative rule is 'when unknown, INCLUDE'. The proposer may add genre-scoped excludeForGenres, but must not flip a section-group's DEFAULT to exclude (that would silently drop context for unclassified goals).
